audit_score_notes: 2 provisional flags: (1) SB 224 (2023) final enrolled form not independently verified against enacted text — $2,500/$5,000 civil penalty enhancement sourced from secondary legal commentary; (2) NRS 612.085 ESD six-factor formulation derived from statute exemption structure and secondary commentary, not DETR primary guidance. All NRS 608.0155 three-part test criteria sourced from primary statute. Hero reused from sibling page (229KB, valid JPEG, passes ≥100KB requirement).

flags.detail: SB 224 (2023) added civil penalty enhancements for worker misclassification in Nevada. The $2,500 (first offence) / $5,000 (subsequent) per-worker framework predates SB 224 under NRS 608.0155. SB 224 layered additional enforcement mechanisms. Final enrolled text of SB 224 not independently verified; penalty amounts and enhancement provisions sourced from Nevada Labor Commissioner secondary guidance and legal commentary.

The Nevada ESD six-factor test for UI classification is derived from the statutory exemption structure in NRS 612.085 and secondary legal commentary. The exact six-factor enumeration (particularly factors 4-6 beyond the NRS 608.0155 overlap) should be confirmed against published DETR guidance or enrolled statutory text before relying on specific factor wording.

inline_visuals.content: Six-row card mapping which classification test applies to each context: wage/hour (NRS 608.0155), minimum wage (economic realities), UI (NRS 612.085 ESD), workers comp (NRS 616B common-law), construction (NRS 624), federal payroll tax (IRS 20-factor). Floated right in section 1.

Visual callout: $2,500 first offence / $5,000 subsequent offence per misclassified worker. NRS 608.0155 civil penalties, SB 224 (2023) enhancements. Amber/orange treatment with statute citation and provisional flag.
